title: LilyGO device UI for LICHEN firmware
description: Why this exists: LilyGO-class Meshtastic-compatible boards are likely first-run contributor/user hardware, and the Zephyr firmware needs an on-device UI path for status, pairing, diagnostics, and basic mesh interaction rather than relying only on serial logs or phone apps. What needs to be done: design and implement the LilyGO UI surface for supported display/button/GPS variants, including boot/status screens, node identity display, radio/link status, neighbor/route summary, message or app notification views, settings affordances, error states, and low-power behavior. Integrate with the existing Zephyr firmware abstractions without hard-coding a single board layout. Acceptance criteria: supported LilyGO boards are enumerated; display/input drivers and board overlays are documented; UI state model and rendering loop are implemented or explicitly scoped into child Beads; simulator or hardware test path is documented; build succeeds for at least one LilyGO target; two code-review passes are completed before closure.
notes: Location/GNSS is firmware-wide, not LilyGO-specific. LilyGO UI must consume the unified location provider tracked by project-LICHEN-zte2 and render fix/source/age/quality from that provider instead of assuming onboard GNSS.
